channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korean President Yoon faces second impeachment vote over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean lawmakers are set to vote for a second time on an impeachment motion against President Yoon Suk Yeol on Saturday, as authorities investigate allegations of rebellion over his controversial Dec.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Drones, planes or UFOs? Americans abuzz over mysterious New Jersey sightings
item.description: CHATHAM, N.J. (AP) — That buzzing coming out of New Jersey? It's unclear if it's drones or something else, but for sure the nighttime sightings are producing tons of talk, a raft of conspiracy theories and craned necks looking skyward.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting suspect fled New York via train, not by bus as originally thought: reports
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, the man accused of fatally shooting UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, is believed to have taken a train out of Manhattan, not a bus as originally thought.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ACLU: Officials should enact law 'firewall' ahead of Trump 2nd term
item.description: ACLU leaders told press Wednesday that state and city governments should prepare for the implementation of ideas from Project 2025.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Winter weather safety: Why bulky coats could be dangerous for children in car seats
item.description: Here are some winter weather tips for drivers, including some on how to make sure children stay safe and warm while in their car seats.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Manchin: Biden 'went completely insane on me' over American Rescue Plan opposition
item.description: “I was going to kill the whole bill. Then the president went completely insane on me,” Manchin said in an interview with news outlet Semafor that was published on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Woman who falsely accused Duke lacrosse players of rape in 2006 publicly admits she lied
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The woman who in 2006 falsely accused three Duke University lacrosse players of raping her — making national headlines that stirred tensions about race, class and the privilege of college athletes — has admitted publicly for the first time that she made up the story.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California DMV apologizes for Cybertruck's offensive license plate
item.description: On Thursday, the license plate in question, which was on a Tesla Cybertruck, was spotlighted in a post to X by StopAntisemitism, a group “dedicated to fighting anti-Semitism.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court will take up a challenge related to California's tough vehicle emissions standards
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a business-backed appeal that could make it easier to challenge federal regulations, acting in a dispute related to California's nation-leading standards for vehicle emissions.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas AG sues New York doctor for providing abortion pills across state lines
item.description: Both surgical and medication abortions are banned in Texas, and Friday’s lawsuit is the first legal test to see what happens when state abortion laws conflict with one another.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US military flies American released from Syrian prison to Jordan, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has transported out of Syria an American who had disappeared seven months ago into former President Bashar Assad’s notorious prison system and was among the thousands released this week by rebels, U.S. officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How to write a holiday rom-com for TV, according to the experts
item.description: Have you ever watched a holiday rom-com on Hallmark Channel or Lifetime — or any of the other many networks and streamers that now air them — and thought, “I could write that”? It's harder than you may think — but just as fun.
item.dc:creator: ALICIA RANCILIO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden's commutation in 'kids for cash' scandal angers some Pennsylvania families
item.description: A judge who helped orchestrate a scheme to send children to for-profit jails in exchange for kickbacks was among the 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted by President Joe Biden this week.
item.dc:creator: Michael Rubinkam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taylor Swift visits patients at Children's Mercy Hospital in Kansas City
item.description: Fresh off her final Era's Tour stop Sunday in Canada, Taylor Swift made a surprise visit at Children's Mercy Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says GOP will push to eliminate daylight saving time
item.description: President-elect Trump on Friday said Republicans would push to eliminate daylight saving time, calling it "inconvenient" and "costly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medical professionals bemoan 'out of control' costs, insurance policies
item.description: In the wake of UnitedHealthCare CEO Brian Thompson's death, an outpouring of anger has been directed toward health care providers.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Krispy Kreme offering green doughnuts for 2 days only
item.description: The chain also has a limited time “Merry Grinchmas Doughnut Collection” to celebrate the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Health insurance stocks have fallen since UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: Major health insurance carriers have seen their stocks tumble amid outrage with the nation's health care industry.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Capitol rioter who tried to join Russian army is sentenced to prison for probation violation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Dallas man who tried to fly overseas to join the Russian military and fight against Ukraine was sentenced on Friday to six months in prison for violating the terms of his probation for storming the U.S. Capitol four years ago. Kevin Loftus, a 56-year-old veteran of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman who accused Duke lacrosse players of rape admits she lied
item.description: A woman who accused three former Duke University men’s lacrosse players of rape nearly two decades ago admitted she lied about the allegations and asked them for forgiveness.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Albertsons sues Kroger for failing to win approval of their proposed supermarket merger
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons' plan for the largest U.S. supermarket merger in history crumbled Wednesday, with Albertsons pulling out of the $24.6 billion deal and the two companies accusing each other of not doing enough to push their proposed alliance through.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
